rulings.Case Title: MARK BELANGER VS. WINE.COM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S Motion To Dismiss Action. Motion to Dismiss denied. The Stipulation for Settlement (Herman Dec. Ex. A) states "2. To plaintiff for physical injuries induced by emotional distress subject to a 1099." The Court finds that the Stipulation characterized the injuries as physical. The 1099 language does not mandate the issuance of a 1099, , but that it is subject to 1099 treatment only if required. The IRS Form 1099 (Ex. E, Murphy Dec.) appears to indicate that physical injuries do not require issuance of a 1099. Defendant should issue payment accordingly. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA SCOTT VS. CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S Motion For Sanctions. The motion is denied. First, it is unclear whether defendant complied with the safe harbor provisions of CCP 128.7 and gave plaintiff an opportunity to withdraw or amend the pleading. See CCP 128.7(c)(1). Second, defendant fails to show that the allegations are frivolous as opposed to an honest mistake. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA AKHRAMENKO VS. NASIR PATEL DBA PARK HOTEL AND TRUST FAMILY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Verified Responses To Disco Requests
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF VICTORIA AKHRAMENKO'S Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Discovery Requests. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. It appears that the motion was made more than 45 days after responses were served on plaintiff's attorney who subsequently withdrew from the case. If the verification was not provided until after the motion was filed then the 45 days would not run until the verification was received. (Garber & Assocs. v. Eskandarian (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 813, 817 n. 4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN HOME REALTY NETWORK, INC. VS. DANIEL CASABONNE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT DANIEL CASABONNE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend. Pursuant to CCP 430.10(e), the court will sustain Defendant's demurrer with ten days leave to amend on the grounds that Plaintiff has failed to plead facts which sufficiently establish the existence of a valid contract between the parties. The terms of the Referral Agreement required execution by Defendant. Neither the case law cited by Plaintiff, nor the references to the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act CC § 1633.1 et seq. support Plaintiff's position that the typed words, "accepted by telephone," constitute an electronic signature. Because Plaintiff has not pled facts that Defendant executed the agreement, Plaintiff has failed to state facts sufficient enough to bring a cause of action for breach of contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: LACHELE BRADFORD ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF VS. ADRIENNE GAIL BLALOCK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY, FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion granted without leave to amend. Pursuant to CCP 435 and 436 the court will grant the Fidelity Defendants' motion to strike the eleventh and twelfth causes of actions in the First Amended Complaint. The court will strike the cause of action for attorney fees because Plaintiff has failed to allege facts which establish the existence of an agreement entitling her to attorney fees, Plaintiff has failed to cite a statute under which she may bring her action for attorney fees, and the tort of another doctrine does not apply in this case. The court will strike the twelfth cause of action for a constructive trust because Plaintiff has merely made a request for a remedy without pleading the elements which would establish entitlement to a constructive trust. Furthermore, Plaintiff admits in 47 of the First Amended Complaint that the Fidelity Defendants are no longer in possession of a res upon which a constructive trust may be imposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.et al, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. BARBARA BERWICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier-Ca, Llcs Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 21, 2015, Line 14, APPELLANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RAISER-CA, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration is denied. The delegation clause is not enforceable because it is not clear and unmistakable. See Tiri v. Lucky Chances, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 231, 242. The contract is contradictory. Page 12 of the contract indicates that the arbitrator decides arbitrability. Page 17 states that the court will decide the issue. Plaintiff establishes that the contract is unconscionable. Gentry v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 443, 466-473 (reversed on other grounds in Iskanian, 59 Cal.4th at 366) explains that procedural unconscionability exists despite an opt out provision. The contract is also substantively unconscionable. The contract precludes representative actions, including PAGA claims. The contract carves out intellectual property claims and Uber-not a driver-is likely to prosecute such claims. The confidentiality clause favors Uber as a repeat player in the arbitration arena. The unilateral modification provision also unfairly favors Uber. Plaintiff's waiver argument has less merit because the contract expressly allows for administrative actions rather than arbitration. The court notes, however, that allowing the administrative process to go forward and then switching to arbitration to re-litigate the matter is totally anathema to the purpose of arbitration, which is to provide a quick resolution of a matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
